Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Further Answers To Discovery Request And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-09-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Cross-Defendant Carol J. Flynn Inc.'s Motion To Compel Further Answers To Discovery Requests is granted in part and denied in part.
The motion is granted as to Interrogatory Numbers 2-4 and 6-14 as Defendant's objections are overruled and ordered stricken. The responses otherwise may be adequate.
The motion is granted as to Request For Production Of Documents Numbers 1-11 as the objections to the requests as they seek premature production of expert or consultant files are overruled and ordered stricken. Attorney client and work product objections are sustained. Any documents for which privilege is claimed shall be identified in a privilege log.
As to the Form interrogatories, the motion is granted as to Numbers 15.1, 16.2, 16.3, and 16.8 as the objections are overruled and ordered stricken. Defendant is ordered to respond further.
The motion is granted as to Numbers 16.1. The objection is overruled. The response, as limited, is inadequate.
The motion is denied as to Number 17.1.
The motion is granted as to Number 50.1 as the objections are overruled and ordered stricken. The response otherwise may be adequate.
Further responses shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order.
Sanctions are denied. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.
